untranslatable_reason: Signal requires external economic data (monthly unemployment figure and market expectations comparison). This is a macro-economic event-driven signal that cannot be expressed using only price-based technical indicators. The core condition 'market underestimated unemployment by at least 0.2%' requires real-time access to: (1) actual unemployment data release, (2) pre-release market consensus forecasts, and (3) calculation of the difference. None of these are available in the allowed indicator set, which is limited to price, volume, and technical analysis indicators.
translation_notes: This is an EVENT-based signal requiring fundamental/macro data integration. To implement this signal, the backtest engine would need: (1) a calendar of unemployment report dates (first Friday of each month), (2) actual vs. expected unemployment data feeds, (3) logic to detect when actual < expected by >= 0.2%, and (4) conditional entry/exit logic tied to those events. The placeholder entry condition uses prev_close comparison as a dummy condition only—the real trigger must be the economic event itself.
